associated_id_checker.description: Validates whether a given disease name is recognized by the OpenTarget API. HUMAN-SPECIFIC: Only applicable when working with human disease contexts. Skip this checker for non-human organisms.

input_params.description: The name of the disease to validate (e.g., 'rheumatoid arthritis'). This parameter is used to check if the disease is recognized by the OpenTarget API. MUST NOT be empty or null.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if the specified disease name is recognized by the OpenTarget API; otherwise, False.

associated_id_checker.description: Validates whether the cell type and model context are available for PINNACLE PPI analysis. HUMAN-SPECIFIC: This checker validates human cell types and human gene embeddings. Do NOT use for non-human organisms. If your query involves non-human genes (e.g., yeast), map to human orthologs first before using this tool.

input_params.description: The name of the disease to validate (e.g., 'rheumatoid arthritis'). MUST NOT be empty or null.
The name of the cell type to check for gene availability (e.g., 'b_cell'). MUST NOT be empty, null, or None as the checker explicitly validates against None values.
Optional. A list of HUMAN gene names to validate for embedding availability. If empty or null, only disease and cell_type context will be validated.
The name of the model to use for validation. Case-insensitive.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if the disease and cell_type context are valid. If gene_list is provided, also validates embedding availability for those genes; otherwise, skips gene validation.

associated_id_checker.description: Validates whether the tissue context and gene names are valid for HumanBase analysis. HUMAN-SPECIFIC: HumanBase only contains human tissue networks. Gene names must be human gene symbols. Do NOT pass non-human gene names directly — map to human orthologs first if working with other organisms.

input_params.description: Optional. A list of HUMAN gene symbols to validate for HumanBase analysis.
The name of the tissue context to validate (e.g., 'brain', 'heart', 'liver', 'blood', 'muscle', 'kidney'). Must match HumanBase supported tissue contexts. MUST NOT be empty.
Optional parameter for limiting results. Defaults to 5 if not specified. IMPORTANT: top_n > 10 would be time-consuming and not recommended.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if the tissue context is valid. If gene_list is provided, also validates those genes; otherwise, skips gene validation. Returns detailed information about any invalid genes or unsupported tissues found.

associated_id_checker.description: Validates whether gene names are recognized human gene symbols compatible with Enrichr-based analysis tools. HUMAN-SPECIFIC: Enrichr databases use human gene nomenclature. If your genes are from another organism (e.g., yeast, mouse), map them to human orthologs first using the appropriate ortholog mapping tool, then pass the human orthologs to this checker. Non-human gene symbols will be flagged with a warning but will not block execution.

input_params.description: Optional. A list of HUMAN gene symbols to validate (e.g., ['TP53', 'MDM2']). If empty or null, skips validation.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if validation is skipped (empty gene_list) or if all gene names in the list are valid and can be processed by Enrichr-based analysis tools; otherwise, False. Returns detailed information about any invalid gene names found.

associated_id_checker.description: Validates whether concept names are recognized within Compass. HUMAN-SPECIFIC: Compass concepts are derived from human single-cell data. Only use when the query involves Compass-based predictions.

input_params.description: The exact user query as originally provided by the user. Copy and paste the full query without modifications. MUST NOT be empty.
A list of concept names to validate against Compass's predefined concepts. MUST contain at least one concept name.
Number of retry attempts for LLM validation. Defaults to 3 if not specified.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if all provided concept names exist in Compass; otherwise, False.

associated_id_checker.description: Validates whether gene names are recognized by SGD (yeast) or MyGene.info (human). MULTI-SPECIES: This checker supports both yeast and human gene validation depending on the 'organism' parameter. Use organism='yeast' for S. cerevisiae genes, organism='human' for reverse lookups.

input_params.description: Optional. A list of gene names to validate. For yeast: standard names (e.g., 'RAD51') or systematic names (e.g., 'YER095W'). For human: symbols like 'CDK1'.
Which organism the gene names belong to: 'yeast' for S. cerevisiae genes (default), 'human' for H. sapiens genes in reverse lookups.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if all gene names are valid and recognized; otherwise, False. Returns detailed information about any unrecognized gene names.

associated_id_checker.description: Validates the context for TranscriptformerEmbeddingTool. HUMAN-SPECIFIC: Transcriptformer models are trained on human single-cell data. Gene names must be human gene symbols or Ensembl IDs. Do NOT pass non-human gene identifiers.

input_params.description: The user's original query, used to infer the best context.
The disease state context (e.g., 'control', 'disease'). Can not be empty or null/None.
A list of HUMAN gene identifiers (symbols or Ensembl IDs). Can be None.
The name of the disease to load embeddings for. Can not be empty or null/None.

associated_id_checker.returns: True if the context is valid, False otherwise. If False, a message with a suggested valid context is returned.
